title: centaurea × cladovae
intro: Centaurea × cladovae, also known as the hybrid cornflower or Russian cornflower, is a plant species that belongs to the aster family and is a hybrid of Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea jacea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Centaurea × cladovae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of Centaurea × cladovae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
